program_chi: 城市研究社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Urban Studies 1. Major Programme: With its interdisciplinary
approach, the Urban Studies Programme aims to overcome the compartmentalised
thinking in separate fields. Students are expected to develop a capacity to
integrate knowledge, skills, values and attitudes in comprehending and
solving urban issues. Graduates will have acquired the
ability to: Knowledge (1) explain, analyse and evaluate multi-scalar and multi-dimensional factors creating and influencing urban spaces and places
over time that shape and affect people’s needs at different geographical
levels; (2) appraise the roles of the governments, the market
and the civil society at different geographical scales and over time in
shaping the courses of urbanisation or urban transformation and to reflect on
their impacts on the well-being of people, place and planet; (3) compare and contrast different modes of
producing, consuming and distributing resources and generalise their impacts
on the ecological landscape and the consequent well-being of individual and
collective actors; (4) distinguish and reflect on various patterns of
integration of cities in regional and global networks; and (5) identify specifics of urban development in Hong
Kong and mainland China and develop an ability to create and design a
sustainable future for the region. Skills (1) apply qualitative and quantitative methods to
analyse and differentiate the quality of spaces and places; (2) develop independent thinking, collaborative
learning, communication and presentation skills and to apply them in team
work setting to nurture co-learning and co-creating capacity; and (3) use visualisation techniques, spatial thinking
and design and computer skills (GIS, remote sensing, environmental
simulation, CAD, etc.) to comprehend, co-develop creative and
inter-disciplinary solutions to resolve multi-dimensional urban problems. Values and Attitudes (1) value collaboration, interdependence, empathy and
integrity to build an inclusive urbanism that respects natural ecology and people’s
dignity; (2) apply their global outlook, social-ecological
awareness and professional ethics in their service to society; (3) appreciate the importance of preserving the
character of place and its cultural value; and (4) develop a capacity to respect the diversity of human
life and the environment through life-long learning. 2. Minor
Programme: Upon the completion of the Minor
Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental
issues related to urban studies such as various factors influencing
multi-scalar and multi-dimensional urban development, planning and
governance, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient
exposure and at a suitable level of skill competency to allow further study
in this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career. While most skills and attitudes
expected of a graduate would have been developed through the Major Programme,
graduates who have been awarded a Minor will have developed the skills and
attitudes that are related specifically to this Minor Programme.

Urban Studies Urban Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major

Students are required to complete
a minimum of 71 units of courses as follows:
